We need a 5-letter word containing l in either 1 or 3, e in 1-4, no a,p,b,o,k,j,w.

Third guess: jewel -> j e w e l gives feedback j red, e green, w red, e green, l green. So e is green at positions 2 and 4 simultaneously? That conflicts because we can't have e in both positions. Wait "jewel": positions: j(1) e(2) w(3) e(4) l(5). Feedback: j red, e green, w red, e green, l green. That says e is green at pos2 and pos4, l green at pos5. This implies e appears twice? but target word is 5 letters single letters may repeat. But earlier from apple we had e present but not 5. But from jewel, l at 5 is green, OK. e must be at pos2 and pos4: meaning the target word has two e's, at positions 2 and 4. That is allowed. We also know j,w,b,o,k,a,p not present.

So target word currently: _ e _ e l with other letters unknown but not containing banned list. The remaining positions 1,3 to fill with letters not banned. Also e appears twice. Word must be a valid English word. 5 letters: ? e ? e l. Candidates: "pezel"? no. "b e n e l"? "benel" not. "d e n e l"? "denel" not common. "sequel"? six letters. But maybe "bezel"? that's b e z e l. But b is banned. "medal"? m e d e l -> that fits pattern ? e ? e l. m e d e l. letters m,d not banned. Also we know l must be at pos5; e at pos2 and 4; pos1 m; pos3 d. This is "medal". Are m or d banned? No, not in list. So medal is candidate. Also "segel"? not word. "kezel"? not.
